requirements.name: Government-issued photo ID
requirements.description: A driver's license, state ID, or passport for the person applying.

requirements.name: Social Security number
requirements.description: Your Social Security card works, and so do a W-2, a pay stub with the full number, or a letter from Social Security. Whatever you have that shows the number — a full list is just below.
accepts: Original Social Security card
Official letter from the Social Security Administration (SSA)
A recent W-2 or 1099 tax form showing the full SSN
A pay stub that shows the full SSN
Military ID card, or military discharge papers (these are sometimes called a DD-214)
A Medi-Cal or Medicare card, if yours has the Social Security number printed on it (some do, some don't)

requirements.name: Recent proof of income
requirements.description: Proof of income from the last 30 days: pay stubs, an employer letter, or your most recent tax return if self-employed.

requirements.name: Proof of address
requirements.description: Something that shows where you live: a utility bill, a lease, or recent mail with your name and address.
accepts: A utility bill
A lease or rental agreement
Recent mail with your name and address

requirements.name: Self-employment business records
requirements.description: Records of your business income and expenses, such as a profit and loss statement or bank statements.
requirements.condition_note: if anyone in your household is self-employed.

requirements.name: Benefit award letters
requirements.description: Any letter or statement that shows benefits someone in your household gets and how much — for example, a disability or unemployment notice, or a record of child support payments. Whatever you have that shows the amount works.
requirements.condition_note: if anyone in your household receives disability, unemployment, child support, or other benefits.

requirements.name: Government-issued photo ID
requirements.description: A driver's license, state ID, or passport for the person applying.

requirements.name: Social Security number
requirements.description: Your Social Security card works, and so do a W-2, a pay stub with the full number, or a letter from Social Security. Whatever you have that shows the number — a full list is just below.
accepts: Original Social Security card
Official letter from the Social Security Administration (SSA)
A recent W-2 or 1099 tax form showing the full SSN
A pay stub that shows the full SSN
Military ID card, or military discharge papers (these are sometimes called a DD-214)
A Medi-Cal or Medicare card, if yours has the Social Security number printed on it (some do, some don't)

requirements.name: Recent proof of income
requirements.description: Proof of income from the last 30 days: pay stubs, an employer letter, or your most recent tax return if self-employed.

requirements.name: Proof of address
requirements.description: Something that shows where you live: a utility bill, a lease, or recent mail with your name and address.
accepts: A utility bill
A lease or rental agreement
Recent mail with your name and address

requirements.name: Birth certificate for a child
requirements.description: A birth certificate or other document showing the child's name, date of birth, and relationship to you.
requirements.condition_note: if you are applying for a child.

requirements.name: Self-employment business records
requirements.description: Records of your business income and expenses, such as a profit and loss statement or bank statements.
requirements.condition_note: if anyone in your household is self-employed.

requirements.name: Benefit award letters
requirements.description: Any letter or statement that shows benefits someone in your household gets and how much — for example, a disability or unemployment notice, or a record of child support payments. Whatever you have that shows the amount works.
requirements.condition_note: if anyone in your household receives disability, unemployment, child support, or other benefits.

requirements.name: Government-issued photo ID
requirements.description: A driver's license, state ID, or passport for the person applying.

requirements.name: Social Security number
requirements.description: For each adult in your household: a Social Security card, W-2, or pay stub with the full number all work. A full list of what counts is just below.
accepts: Original Social Security card
Official letter from the Social Security Administration (SSA)
A recent W-2 or 1099 tax form showing the full SSN
A pay stub that shows the full SSN
Military ID card, or military discharge papers (these are sometimes called a DD-214)
A Medi-Cal or Medicare card, if yours has the Social Security number printed on it (some do, some don't)

requirements.name: Recent proof of income
requirements.description: Proof of income from the last 30 days for anyone in your household who earns money — pay stubs work, or last year's tax return. If you're the only one who works, that's just your own.

requirements.name: Most recent energy or utility bill
requirements.description: Your most recent energy or utility bill — electric, gas, or whatever heats your home. It shows the account number and service address the program looks for. This same bill often counts as proof of where you live for other programs too.

requirements.name: Benefit award letters
requirements.description: Any letter or statement that shows benefits someone in your household gets and how much — for example, a disability or unemployment notice, or a record of child support payments. Whatever you have that shows the amount works.
requirements.condition_note: if anyone in your household receives disability, unemployment, child support, or other benefits.

eitc.label: EITC
eitc.program_note: EITC is a tax credit you claim on your tax return when you file, not a separate application. The documents below are what you bring to your tax preparer or use to file.

requirements.name: Government-issued photo ID
requirements.description: A driver's license, state ID, or passport for the person filing.

requirements.name: Social Security numbers for you and each child you claim
requirements.description: A document showing the Social Security number for yourself and each child you are claiming. This is a tax credit claimed on your return, so the IRS needs SSNs for everyone listed.
accepts: Original Social Security card
Official letter from the Social Security Administration (SSA)
A recent W-2 or 1099 tax form showing the full SSN
A pay stub that shows the full SSN
Military ID card, or military discharge papers (these are sometimes called a DD-214)
A Medi-Cal or Medicare card, if yours has the Social Security number printed on it (some do, some don't)

requirements.name: Earned income documents (W-2 or 1099 forms)
requirements.description: Your W-2 or 1099 forms showing what you earned last year. If any of that was self-employment income, your tax preparer may also ask for the self-employment form that goes with it (Schedule SE).

requirements.name: Proof of a qualifying child (relationship and age)
requirements.description: A birth certificate or official school record that shows the child's name, date of birth, and relationship to you.
requirements.condition_note: if you are claiming a qualifying child.

requirements.name: Prior-year tax return
requirements.description: Your most recent tax return. Helpful but not required. Bring it if you have it.

residency.proof_of_address.divergence_note: CalFresh can accept a utility bill, a lease, or recent mail. If you are also applying for HEAP, a recent energy or utility bill covers both at once.

identity.photo_id.divergence_note: Medi-Cal may accept a signed statement if you do not have a photo ID available. Ask your county office.

program_specific.energy_bill.divergence_note: This same energy or utility bill also works as proof of where you live for CalFresh and Medi-Cal applications, so one copy covers all three.
